findings.finding: The plan claims 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three [location-pinned modules] the same way', but `__main__.py` contains zero references to `adf`, `outbound_fields`, or `comment_limits` — the asserted update sites do not exist.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the outbound_comments.py ownership ambiguity with the parent (epic): either update the epic's S5 AC to reflect that outbound_comments.py sibling-filename refs are eliminated in S4 (not repointed in S5), or remove the 'not already eliminated by S4' conditionality from the leaf and commit to repointing any residual refs in S5 regardless.
evidence: Epic AC (S5 bullet): 'the two non-moved root modules that reference them by sibling filename (`inbound_fields.py`, `outbound_comments.py`) are repointed'
Leaf: 'Any `outbound_comments.py` / `inbound_translate.py` / `reconcile_check.py` adf/comment_limits loader refs **not already eliminated by S4** — grep-confirmed at S5 time; repoint or drop'
findings.finding: The epic's S5 AC names two specific non-moved root modules to repoint by sibling filename — `inbound_fields.py` AND `outbound_comments.py` — as firm S5 deliverables. The leaf makes `outbound_comments.py` conditional ('Any outbound_comments.py / inbound_translate.py / reconcile_check.py adf/comment_limits loader refs not already eliminated by S4 — grep-confirmed at S5 time; repoint or drop'), treating it as potentially out-of-scope if S4 already handled it. The parent's AC specifies it as an S5 deliverable; the leaf's conditioning contradicts that by allowing S5 to skip it.
findings.location: Leaf 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint' section vs. Epic AC: 'the two non-moved root modules that reference them by sibling filename (inbound_fields.py, outbound_comments.py) are repointed'
scenarios: S4 eliminates the outbound_comments.py adf/comment_limits refs as part of its Backend-port rewiring. S5 greps, finds zero hits, and skips outbound_comments.py. The epic AC says S5 repointed it — which is now false. The epic's attested plan-review is stale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the epic (bbf1-82e1-cf9d-494a) AC for S5 to replace '~29 test patch strings' with the corrected characterization (zero string patches; ~18 path-constant files + ~18 dotted-import files migrated) before this leaf is merged, so the parent's attested plan matches the leaf's actual delivery.
evidence: Leaf: 'The first draft's "~29 `mock.patch` strings" was wrong; there are zero `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")` string patches'
Epic AC: 'all ~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims (S5)'
Leaf reframes migration scope as: ~23 files use path-agnostic patch.object (not migrated), ~18 files use path constants (category 2), ~18 files use dotted imports (category 3)
findings.finding: The leaf contradicts the parent (epic) AC on the character and count of the test migration target. The epic's S5 AC says 'all ~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims'; the leaf asserts 'there are zero `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")` string patches' and replaces the ~29-string-patch deliverable with a two-category framework (~18 path constants + ~18 dotted imports). The leaf is redefining what the parent specifies as the test migration deliverable. Per the conflict rule, the parent's AC wins — if the patch-string count/characterization has been corrected by implementation-time grep, the parent's AC must be updated first; only then can the leaf diverge from '~29 string patches.'
findings.location: Leaf Testing section / AC line 3 ('category-2/3 test targets reference the new adapters.jira.* paths') vs. Epic AC: '~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims (S5)'
scenarios: A reviewer checking the leaf against the epic AC for '~29 test patch strings' finds the leaf's deliverable does not match — the parent AC is stale/wrong per the leaf's own claim, but the parent hasn't been updated.
If the grep-count correction is wrong (some string patches do exist), the unedited parent AC provides the correct specification but the leaf will skip them.
findings.suggested_fix: Raise a plan edit on the epic's S5 AC bullet to read: 'all ~18 path-constant test files and ~18 dotted-import test files (categories 2 and 3 per the S5 grep taxonomy) are migrated to adapters.jira.* paths with no re-export shims; the ~23 path-agnostic patch.object sites are confirmed green without edit.'  Then re-attest the epic before landing S5.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the plan title to accurately reflect the actual test migration work (e.g., 'migrate ~18 path constants + ~18 dotted imports atomically') so the title and Testing section are coherent.
evidence: Title: 'migrate ~29 patch strings atomically'
Testing section: 'the first draft's ~29 mock.patch strings was wrong; there are zero mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") string patches'
findings.finding: The plan title says 'migrate ~29 patch strings atomically' but the Testing section explicitly and emphatically corrects this to ZERO string-level mock.patch calls ('there are zero mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") string patches'). An implementer reading the summary/title would expect to hunt for and rewrite ~29 patch strings — work that the body says does not exist.
findings.location: Title vs. Testing section ('~23 files use patch.object … path-agnostic; unaffected')
scenarios: An implementer scopes the task from the title, allocates time to find/rewrite 29 string patches, and discovers none exist — scope mismatch causes confusion about what the real work is.
A reviewer approves based on the title's framing and misunderstands the actual change surface.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to something like: 'Relocate acli*/adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits into adapters/jira/ + teach loader; migrate category-2/3 test references (path constants + dotted imports) atomically'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the plan title to accurately reflect the actual migration: e.g. '…migrate category-2/3 test path constants and dotted imports atomically' (replacing the stale '~29 patch strings' claim that the body itself retracts).
evidence: Title: 'migrate ~29 patch strings atomically'
Body Testing section: 'the first draft's "~29 `mock.patch` strings" was wrong; there are **zero** `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")` string patches'
Body describes actual work as rewriting ~18 path constants (category 2) and ~18 dotted imports (category 3), not string-based mock.patch calls
findings.finding: The plan's title says 'migrate ~29 patch strings atomically', but the body explicitly corrects this, stating 'there are **zero** `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")` string patches' and that the actual migration set is category-2/3 (path constants + dotted imports). The stale title misrepresents what the plan actually does, which would mislead a reader (or automated agent) about the nature of the test-reference migration work.
findings.location: Title: '…migrate ~29 patch strings atomically' vs. Testing section: 'there are **zero** `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")` string patches'
scenarios: An agent or implementer reading only the title allocates effort toward finding and migrating mock.patch string arguments, finds none, and may incorrectly conclude the test migration is already complete or trivial — missing the real category-2/3 work.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '…migrate ~29 patch strings atomically' in the title with '…migrate ~36 category-2/3 test path constants and dotted imports atomically' (or similar wording consistent with the body's corrected characterization).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State an explicit default for the S4-residual refs in `outbound_comments.py` / `inbound_translate.py` / `reconcile_check.py`: specify whether each surviving ref is unconditionally repointed (if the call-site survives S4) or unconditionally dropped (if S4 already removes it), rather than leaving 'repoint or drop' as an executor decision.
evidence: 'repoint or drop' is an unresolved binary design choice deferred to the executor
The plan elsewhere is explicit about every other reference's disposition (repoint vs leave); this is the only exception
ADR Decision 4 prohibits re-export shims but does not resolve whether a caller should be removed or redirected
findings.finding: The plan defers a binary design choice — 'repoint or drop' — for any S4-residual loader refs in `outbound_comments.py` / `inbound_translate.py` / `reconcile_check.py` to the executor at implementation time, without stating a default. 'Drop' eliminates functionality; 'repoint' preserves it. These are not equivalent outcomes and the plan should decide.
findings.location: Edit-set bullet: 'Any outbound_comments.py / inbound_translate.py / reconcile_check.py adf/comment_limits loader refs NOT already eliminated by S4 — grep-confirmed at S5 time; repoint or drop.'
scenarios: An executor who drops a ref eliminates a call-site that another executor would have repointed, producing a silent behavior difference that the suite may not catch if the path is only exercised by S4-eliminated tests.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'repoint or drop' with: 'If a ref in these files survives S4 (grep-confirmed), repoint it to `adapters.jira.*`; if S4 eliminates it, no edit is needed. Do not drop a surviving ref.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a sequencing note in an existing AC) that explicitly records the Teeth test result: e.g., 'At least one deliberately-stale category-2 path constant is staged, the suite is confirmed RED on that ref, and the stale constant is then corrected before the final green run — evidenced by a CI log link or a noted step in the PR description.'
evidence: 'leave one category-2 path constant at the old path and confirm that test errors' appears only in Testing, not in any AC
AC5 only checks 'make lint && make typecheck && make test all green' — it is satisfiable without running the Teeth step
No sequencing AC separates a deliberate-RED phase from the final-GREEN phase
findings.finding: The 'Teeth' test (deliberately leave one category-2 path constant at the old path, confirm the test errors RED) has no corresponding AC or sequencing statement, so the final suite-green AC (AC5) can be satisfied without the Teeth test ever executing. There is no criterion that proves the red signal was observed and then resolved.
findings.location: Testing section, 'Teeth' bullet; Acceptance Criteria AC5.
scenarios: An executor skips the Teeth step and submits a green suite; the structural validation that category-2 tests actually detect stale paths is never demonstrated, leaving a latent gap in the test oracle.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the structural gate and grep sweep to cover `docs/`, `*.md`, and `AGENTS.md` for old root-path references to moved modules, and annotate/update ADR 0035 to reflect the migration is complete after S5 lands.
evidence: 'grep finds zero references to the old root paths of the moved modules in src/ and tests/'
ABSENCE: no mention of grepping docs/, *.md, or AGENTS.md for stale module-path references
ADR 0035 is referenced throughout the plan as the governing decision but no update to it is planned
findings.finding: The structural gate greps only `src/` and `tests/` for stale references to moved module paths, leaving `docs/`, `*.md` files, and `AGENTS.md` unchecked — any documentation that references the old root-level paths (`rebar_reconciler.acli`, `rebar_reconciler.adf`, etc.) or the ADR 0035 'pending' state would be stranded after the move.
findings.location: Testing section / Structural gate bullet; Acceptance Criteria bullet 3
scenarios: A contributor reading ADR 0035 post-S5 sees the old module layout described as 'to be done' with no completion marker.
An AGENTS.md or onboarding doc referencing `rebar_reconciler.adf` or `rebar_reconciler.acli` at the root misleads new contributors.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a grep step: `grep -r 'rebar_reconciler\.acli\|rebar_reconciler\.adf\|rebar_reconciler\.outbound_fields\|rebar_reconciler\.comment_limits' docs/ *.md AGENTS.md` to the structural gate, and add an AC line: 'ADR 0035 is updated or annotated to mark the adapter migration as complete.'

File is at src/rebar/_engine/ (one level above rebar_reconciler/) — not naturally caught by a rebar_reconciler/-scoped grep

An implementor following the plan's narrative edit-set would not know to update this file.
findings.location: Context / Problem — 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint' edit-set
scenarios: Implementor follows the plan's named edit-set, misses jira-capability-probe.py, moves acli.py, and the probe script breaks at runtime with ImportError.
The CI zero-hit grep gate catches it only if the implementor remembers to run `grep -r 'rebar_reconciler.acli' src/` broadly rather than scoping to rebar_reconciler/.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the edit-set: after moving `outbound_fields.py` to `adapters/jira/`, update its internal `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')` (line 69) to `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adapters/jira/adf.py')` so the root-anchored `_loader._PACKAGE_DIR` lookup resolves correctly.
evidence: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_fields.py:69: `_AdfModule = lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')`
_loader.py:56: `path = _PACKAGE_DIR / filename` where `_PACKAGE_DIR = Path(__file__).parent` (the root reconciler dir, not adapters/jira/)

Plan: 'pass a relative subpath for the three moved location-pinned modules — lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, "adapters/jira/adf.py")' — but this describes the external callers' side, not the moved outbound_fields.py's own internal call
findings.finding: After `outbound_fields.py` is moved to `adapters/jira/`, its internal call `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')` (line 69) will resolve via `_loader._PACKAGE_DIR / 'adf.py'` — which is the *root* reconciler directory, not `adapters/jira/`. Since `adf.py` will no longer exist at root, this call will raise `ImportError`. The plan enumerates the external references to `outbound_fields` but does not name this intra-file lazy_load update within the moved module itself.
findings.location: Edit-set / Scope section ('Loader' paragraph and 'Surviving intra-adapter references'); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_fields.py:69
scenarios: After moving outbound_fields.py to adapters/jira/, any code path that exercises _load_adf() inside outbound_fields will raise ImportError because _PACKAGE_DIR/'adf.py' no longer exists.
findings.suggested_fix: In the moved `adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py` line 69, change `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')` to `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adapters/jira/adf.py')`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the `Path(__file__).parent / '_loader.py'` self-bootstrap path in `outbound_fields.py` and `outbound_comments.py` (and any other moved modules containing the same pattern) to navigate back to the package root after the move (e.g., `Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / '_loader.py'`, or preferably replace the pattern with an absolute reference that does not depend on `__file__`'s parent directory).
evidence: outbound_fields.py:43: `_loader_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(_loader_key, Path(__file__).parent / '_loader.py')`
outbound_comments.py:55: identical bootstrap pattern

Plan says: 'pass a relative subpath for the three moved location-pinned modules — lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, "adapters/jira/adf.py")' but does not mention updating the _loader.py bootstrap path within the moved files themselves
ABSENCE: plan has no mention of updating the _loader.py self-bootstrap path expression in outbound_fields.py or outbound_comments.py after they move
findings.finding: The two moved modules that self-bootstrap `_loader.py` by path (`outbound_fields.py` and `outbound_comments.py`) each contain `Path(__file__).parent / '_loader.py'` in their `except ImportError` fallback. After they physically move to `adapters/jira/`, `Path(__file__).parent` resolves to the `adapters/jira/` directory — but `_loader.py` lives at the package root. The plan covers updating `lazy_load(key, 'adf.py')` call-site filenames via relative subpaths, but is entirely silent about this self-bootstrap path inside the moved files, leaving a mechanism gap that causes `ImportError` when these modules are loaded standalone in tests (the exact scenario the fallback is designed to handle).
findings.location: Context / Approach sections; outbound_fields.py:43, outbound_comments.py:55 (the `Path(__file__).parent / '_loader.py'` fallback bootstrap)
scenarios: A test loads `outbound_fields.py` directly via `spec_from_file_location` without package context (the `from rebar_reconciler._loader import lazy_load` fails); the `except ImportError` branch runs `Path(__file__).parent / '_loader.py'` which now resolves to `adapters/jira/_loader.py` — a file that does not exist — causing `ImportError` and a test failure.
findings.suggested_fix: In each moved module's `except ImportError` fallback, change `Path(__file__).parent / '_loader.py'` to `Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / '_loader.py'` (three levels up from `adapters/jira/<module>.py` reaches the `rebar_reconciler/` root where `_loader.py` lives). Add this update explicitly to the Scope and the grep-zero-hits verification gate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the grep completeness gate to include `scripts/` (not just `src/` and `tests/`), and explicitly plan to update or out-of-scope with rationale the `from rebar_reconciler import acli as mod` references and the hardcoded `spec_from_file_location('adf', '…/rebar_reconciler/adf.py')` in `scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh` and `e2e_validation_probe.sh`.
evidence: Plan: 'The change is complete when a grep for the old root paths of the moved modules returns zero hits in `src/` **and** `tests/`'
e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:152: `from rebar_reconciler import acli as mod`
e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:153: `spec_from_file_location('adf', '${_SCRIPTS_DIR}/rebar_reconciler/adf.py')`
e2e_validation_probe.sh:96,121,345,356,455: `from rebar_reconciler import acli as mod`
Plan Approach: 'No re-export shim remains at any old path'
ABSENCE: `scripts/` is never mentioned in the plan's scope, grep gate, or out-of-scope list
findings.finding: The plan's structural completeness gate (grep for old root paths returning zero hits) is scoped to `src/` and `tests/` only, but `scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh` contains ~15 `from rebar_reconciler import acli as mod` references and a hardcoded `spec_from_file_location('adf', '${_SCRIPTS_DIR}/rebar_reconciler/adf.py')` at line 153. After the move, `acli.py` is gone from the package root and no re-export shim is allowed (ADR Decision 4), so these e2e probe scripts will break at runtime — but the plan's grep gate will report a clean pass because `scripts/` is not searched.
findings.location: Approach section ('The change is complete when a grep … returns zero hits in `src/` and `tests/`'); scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:152-153, e2e_validation_probe.sh:96,121,345,356,455
scenarios: A developer runs the Jira e2e pressure-test probe post-migration and gets `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'rebar_reconciler.acli'` because acli.py now lives at adapters/jira/acli.py with no shim.

The plan's structural gate reports zero hits (it only searches src/ and tests/) and CI marks the migration clean, while the e2e probe scripts remain silently broken until an operator runs them against a live Jira environment.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `scripts/` to the grep gate scope and add a task to the edit-set: repoint `from rebar_reconciler import acli as mod` → `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira import acli as mod` (or equivalent) in both probe scripts, and update the hardcoded `adf.py` path at `e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:153` to `adapters/jira/adf.py`. If the probe scripts are intentionally excluded from the migration (e.g., they are considered a separate consumer), state that explicitly in Scope/Out-of-scope with a rationale.

findings.finding: `outbound_fields.py:69` contains `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')` — a root-relative filename passed to the loader. Since `outbound_fields.py` is one of the eight files being moved AND `adf.py` is also being moved, after relocation `_PACKAGE_DIR / 'adf.py'` no longer exists. This internal lazy_load call is not listed in the plan's reference update set.
findings.location: Context/Problem — Loader section; outbound_fields.py:69
findings.suggested_fix: In the Loader section or the 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references' section, add: `outbound_fields.py:69` — `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')` → `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adapters/jira/adf.py')`, since both files will reside in `adapters/jira/` after the move.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (e.g., 'if the suite is red after the move, revert to the pre-move branch tip; no partial intermediate state is recoverable') so the irreversible nature of the no-shim deletion is paired with a documented recovery path.
evidence: 'No behavior changes — a pure relocation + rebind' — but this does not constitute a rollback plan.
'ADR Decision 4' forbids shims, ruling out expand-contract.
Approach: 'One atomic change (the constraints forbid a partial move)' — no staged fallback described.
T4 criterion: 'The remedy is an explicit rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract — acknowledgement alone is insufficient.'
findings.finding: The plan describes no rollback or back-out path for this explicitly breaking, irreversible operation. It removes eight modules from their root paths with no re-export shims (ADR Decision 4), making the change immediately visible to any consumer. If the suite fails mid-migration or a hidden consumer is missed, there is no documented recovery mechanism.
findings.location: Approach section; Acceptance Criteria; Context ('no re-export shim (ADR Decision 4)')
scenarios: S4 lands with residual references the S5 grep sweep misses; mid-migration the suite is red and the team must revert — but there is no documented revert procedure (e.g., restore from branch, no shim, full revert required).
CI catches a hidden consumer after the PR merges; without a rollback plan the team must cut a hotfix with no guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection to Approach: 'This change is atomic and non-reversible in-place (no shims). Back-out = revert the entire PR to the pre-move commit; no partial rollback is possible. Verify the grep gate passes BEFORE merging to reduce revert risk.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the loader's relative-subpath convention for location-pinned adapter modules in AGENTS.md or an ADR 0035 appendix so future adapter contributors know to use `lazy_load(key, 'adapters/<name>/<file>.py')` for non-root modules.
evidence: 'lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, "adapters/jira/adf.py")' — the relative-subpath convention is described in the plan but nowhere designated for persistent documentation
ABSENCE: no mention of updating AGENTS.md or appending to ADR 0035 with the loader's new contract for location-pinned modules
findings.finding: The loader's new relative-subpath contract — that location-pinned adapter modules must be registered with a relative subpath like `'adapters/jira/adf.py'` rather than a bare filename — is not captured in AGENTS.md or an ADR appendix, leaving future contributors who add location-pinned modules in other adapters without discoverable guidance.
findings.location: Context/Problem section — loader update description; Scope section — 'Relative-subpath loader updates'
scenarios: A contributor adding a new location-pinned module in a future adapter (e.g., `adapters/github/`) passes a bare filename to `lazy_load`, which silently resolves to the wrong (root) path, causing a runtime failure that is hard to diagnose.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short note to AGENTS.md (or ADR 0035 §Implementation Notes): 'Location-pinned modules that live outside the reconciler root must be registered with `lazy_load` using a relative subpath, e.g. `lazy_load(_KEY, "adapters/jira/adf.py")`; bare filenames always resolve to the package root.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_error_taxonomy.py:25` and `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_jira_wiring.py:20` to the category-3 test-target list and repoint their imports to the new `adapters.jira.*` paths.
evidence: grep confirms: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_error_taxonomy.py:25: from rebar_reconciler.acli import RetryExhaustedError as FromAcli
grep confirms: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_jira_wiring.py:20: from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import resolve_jira_settings
Plan states category-3 as '~18 files use direct dotted imports from rebar_reconciler.acli… / .adf / .outbound_fields → must be rewritten' — neither file is named
Both imports are of modules in the eight-module move set (acli, acli_subprocess)
findings.finding: The plan's category-3 test-target list ('~18 files use direct dotted imports from rebar_reconciler.acli…/.adf/.outbound_fields') does not enumerate `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_error_taxonomy.py:25` (`from rebar_reconciler.acli import RetryExhaustedError`) or `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_jira_wiring.py:20` (`from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import resolve_jira_settings`). These are plain dotted imports of modules being moved, not `patch.object` or path-constant patterns, and they will break after the move unless updated.
findings.location: Context / Problem — Test surface, Category 3
scenarios: After the move, `test_error_taxonomy.py` fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'rebar_reconciler.acli'` because acli.py no longer lives at the root.
After the move, `test_config_jira_wiring.py` fails with the same error for `acli_subprocess`.
findings.suggested_fix: In test_error_taxonomy.py, change `from rebar_reconciler.acli import RetryExhaustedError` to `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.acli import RetryExhaustedError`. In test_config_jira_wiring.py, change `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import resolve_jira_settings` to `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.acli_subprocess import resolve_jira_settings`.

findings.finding: acli.py:76 (`from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf`) is a surviving dotted-import reference site that the plan's edit-set omits. Since acli.py is one of the eight files being moved to adapters/jira/, after the move this import will refer to a non-existent root-level path and will fail at runtime.
findings.location: Plan section 'Edit-set' / 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet under 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint': `acli.py:76` — `from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf as _text_to_adf` (moves with the `acli*` cluster; becomes an intra-adapter import, same as `acli_cli_ops.py:36`).

findings.location: Context/Problem — 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint'; Scope section; acli.py:76
